all.en: While helping my parents clean the attic, I came across a small wooden box.
all.zh: 我幫父母整理閣樓時，發現了一個小木盒。

all.en: It was tucked behind a stack of yellowing photo albums from the eighties.
all.zh: 它藏在一疊八零年代泛黃相簿的後面。

all.en: Inside, I found a folded letter written in my late grandfather's handwriting.
all.zh: 裡面有一封摺起來的信，是我已過世祖父的字跡。

all.en: The paper was thin and the ink had faded slightly over the years.
all.zh: 信紙很薄，墨水經過多年也已稍微褪色。

all.en: It was addressed to my grandmother during their long-distance courtship.
all.zh: 那是他在遠距離戀愛時寄給祖母的信。

all.en: He wrote about how much he missed her cooking and her laughter.
all.zh: 他在信中寫到，他多麼想念她的料理和她的笑聲。

all.en: He also described his lonely evenings working at the harbor up north.
all.zh: 他也描述了在北部港口工作時，那些孤單的夜晚。

all.en: Reading his words, I could almost hear his voice speaking to me again.
all.zh: 讀著他的字句，我彷彿又能聽見他的聲音。

all.en: My mother cried softly when I quietly handed her the letter to read.
all.zh: 當我安靜地把信遞給媽媽時，她輕聲地哭了。

all.en: We decided to frame the letter and hang it in the hallway downstairs.
all.zh: 我們決定把這封信裱框，掛在樓下的走廊上。

all.en: Some treasures, I realized, are not gold or jewels, but simple words.
all.zh: 我體會到，有些珍寶不是金銀珠寶，而是簡單的文字。

all.en: My father carefully selected a wooden frame with a soft gold border.
all.zh: 爸爸細心地挑選了一個有著淡金色邊框的木框。

all.en: We placed the framed letter beside an old black-and-white wedding photograph.
all.zh: 我們把裱框好的信，擺在一張黑白婚紗照旁邊。

all.en: Every visitor since has paused in the hallway to read my grandfather's words.
all.zh: 從那以後，每位訪客都會在走廊停下來，閱讀祖父的字句。

all.en: My younger cousin asked if she could copy the letter for a school project.
all.zh: 我的小表妹問能不能抄寫這封信當她的學校報告。

all.en: Her teacher later said it was the most heartfelt entry in the whole class.
all.zh: 後來她的老師說，這是全班最有感情的一份作業。

all.en: Inspired by his example, I began writing handwritten letters to distant friends.
all.zh: 受到他的啟發，我開始親手寫信給遠方的朋友。

all.en: My handwriting was clumsy at first, but it slowly grew more confident.
all.zh: 我的字一開始很拙劣，但漸漸變得比較有自信。

all.en: One friend replied with a long letter that arrived three weeks later.
all.zh: 有位朋友回了一封長信，三週後才寄到。

all.en: Reading her words on real paper felt warmer than any text message ever did.
all.zh: 在實際的紙上讀著她的話，比任何訊息都來得溫暖。

all.en: My grandfather, I felt sure, would be quietly proud of this small revival.
all.zh: 我相信祖父若知道這個小小的復古習慣，一定會默默感到驕傲。

all.en: A month after framing the letter, I received a reply from another old friend.
all.zh: 裱框那封信一個月後，我收到了另一位老朋友的回信。

all.en: She had enclosed a faded photograph of us from our high school graduation.
all.zh: 她隨信附上了一張我們高中畢業時，已經泛黃的合照。

all.en: I had not seen that picture in nearly twenty very busy years.
all.zh: 那張照片我大概有將近二十個忙碌的歲月沒見過了。

all.en: My mother helped me find a matching frame for it that afternoon.
all.zh: 那天下午，媽媽幫我找了一個相配的相框來裝它。

all.en: We hung the photo right beside my grandfather's framed handwritten letter.
all.zh: 我們把這張照片掛在祖父親筆信的旁邊。

all.en: The hallway slowly began to feel like a gentle gallery of memories.
all.zh: 那條走廊漸漸變得像一座溫柔的記憶展覽館。

all.en: My father started adding small notes describing each item underneath the frames.
all.zh: 爸爸開始在每個相框下方，加上一張描述物品的小卡片。

all.en: His handwriting was tidy, careful, and surprisingly similar to my grandfather's.
all.zh: 他的字跡工整、細心，而且意外地神似祖父的筆跡。

all.en: My cousin asked if she could contribute a small drawing to the wall.
all.zh: 我表妹問能不能也貢獻一幅小畫，掛在這面牆上。

all.en: Our family wall, I realized, had quietly become a living family album.
all.zh: 我體會到，我們家的這面牆，悄悄地變成了一本活的家庭相簿。

all.en: A few months later, my grandmother visited and slowly walked through our memory hallway.
all.zh: 幾個月後，奶奶來訪，緩緩地走過我們的記憶走廊。

all.en: She stood before her late husband's letter and wiped her eyes very gently.
all.zh: 她站在已故丈夫的信前，輕輕地擦拭著眼角。

all.en: My mother brought her a soft chair so she could sit and read longer.
all.zh: 媽媽搬來一張軟椅，讓她能坐著讀久一點。

all.en: She told stories about each photo with details we had never heard before.
all.zh: 她用我們從未聽過的細節，講述每張照片背後的故事。

all.en: My father quietly recorded her voice with permission on a small handheld recorder.
all.zh: 爸爸在徵得同意後，用一台小錄音機悄悄地錄下她的聲音。

all.en: That afternoon stretched into evening as more relatives gathered in the hallway.
all.zh: 那個下午延續到傍晚，更多親戚聚集到走廊上。

all.en: We ordered noodles and ate them sitting on the carpet beneath the frames.
all.zh: 我們叫了麵食，就坐在相框下方的地毯上吃了起來。

all.en: My grandmother promised to dig out more letters from her own attic boxes.
all.zh: 奶奶答應要從自家閣樓的箱子裡，再翻出更多信件。

all.en: Two weeks later, a thick envelope arrived containing twelve more handwritten letters.
all.zh: 兩週後，一個厚厚的信封寄到了，裡面有十二封親筆信。

all.en: Our hallway, I realized, had become a quiet temple to family memory and love.
all.zh: 我體會到，我們的走廊已變成一座安靜的廟堂，獻給家族的記憶與愛。

all.en: My cousin's small drawing soon inspired several other relatives to send their own artwork.
all.zh: 表妹那張小畫，很快激勵了好幾位親戚寄來他們自己的作品。

all.en: An aunt from Kaohsiung mailed a beautiful watercolor of her childhood home.
all.zh: 住高雄的阿姨寄來了一幅美麗的水彩畫，畫的是她童年的家。

all.en: My uncle in Hualien sent a tiny carved wooden bird wrapped in soft cloth.
all.zh: 花蓮的舅舅寄來了一隻迷你木雕鳥，用柔軟的布包著。

all.en: Our hallway slowly expanded with shelves to display each new family contribution.
all.zh: 我們的走廊逐漸加上層架，用來展示每一件新的家族貢獻。

all.en: My father carefully labeled each item with the donor's name and quiet story.
all.zh: 爸爸細心地為每件物品標上贈送者的姓名和小故事。

all.en: Visitors began calling our home the little family museum of memory and love.
all.zh: 訪客們開始把我們家稱作「家族記憶與愛的小博物館」。

all.en: My grandmother visited again and quietly cried at the size of our gentle collection.
all.zh: 奶奶再度造訪，看到我們收藏規模時，默默地落下了眼淚。

all.en: She added another bundle of grandfather's letters she had recently rediscovered upstairs.
all.zh: 她又加上了一捆她最近在樓上重新找到的祖父信件。

all.en: My cousin started a small notebook recording stories told by each visiting relative.
all.zh: 表妹開始用一本小筆記本，記下每位來訪親戚講述的故事。

all.en: Memory, I realized once more, was the quietest and richest inheritance of all.
all.zh: 我再次體會到，記憶是所有遺產之中最安靜也最豐厚的一份。

all.en: The following spring, my cousin proposed turning the hallway into a small annual family exhibition.
all.zh: 隔年春天，表妹提議把走廊變成每年一度的小型家族展覽。

all.en: Each Lunar New Year, every relative would contribute one new item and one short written story.
all.zh: 每年農曆新年，每位親戚都會貢獻一件新物品和一段簡短的故事。

all.en: We mailed handmade invitations to every branch of our scattered family across the entire island.
all.zh: 我們寄了手作邀請函到全島各地我們四散家族的每一支。

all.en: Over thirty relatives arrived that first New Year, filling our small home with warm laughter.
all.zh: 那年新年有超過三十位親戚到來，為我們的小家注滿溫暖的笑聲。

all.en: My grandmother sat in the place of honor beside my grandfather's framed handwritten letter.
all.zh: 奶奶坐在主位上，緊鄰著祖父裱框的親筆信。

all.en: She told stories late into the night while my cousin recorded every gentle, treasured word.
all.zh: 她說故事說到深夜，表妹則細心錄下每一個溫柔而珍貴的字句。

all.en: My father carefully cataloged every new contribution into a slowly thickening family archive book.
all.zh: 爸爸細心地把每一件新貢獻編入一本逐漸增厚的家族檔案冊中。

all.en: The book sits beside the framed letter, available for any visitor to gently turn through.
all.zh: 這本冊子擺在裱框信件旁邊，歡迎任何訪客輕輕翻閱。

all.en: Last week, a distant young relative flew home and asked to read about his late father.
all.zh: 上週，一位遠方的年輕親戚飛回家鄉，請求閱讀關於他已故父親的篇章。

all.en: He left in tears of quiet gratitude, promising to bring his own children next New Year.
all.zh: 他帶著安靜感恩的淚水離開，承諾明年新年要帶自己的孩子一同回來。
